north_stars.name: Serve No Master
north_stars.description: Building sustainable autonomy by creating passive revenue streams from assets and partnerships, enabling you to make decisions based on your values rather than client demands, employer constraints, or financial desperation.

north_stars.name: Acquire & Leverage Assets
north_stars.description: Building sustainable income by strategically acquiring underutilized or undervalued assets (courses, databases, systems, IP, traffic, audiences) and deploying them across multiple partners, channels, and markets to generate recurring revenue without proportional increases in time or energy.

north_stars.name: Protect Your Time & Energy
north_stars.description: Ruthlessly choosing activities, relationships, and opportunities based on whether they protect or deplete your energy, since time and energy are your most valuable and finite assets -- more valuable than money itself.

north_stars.name: Adopt Investor Mindset
north_stars.description: The foundational shift from employee thinking to investor thinking, treating time, energy, and creativity as capital to deploy strategically toward high-return activities.

concepts.name: The Giving FUNnel
concepts.description: A framework for turning high-intent prospects into pre-sold leads and then into a steady pipeline of first meetings (coffee dates) by leading with value instead of neediness. Built on four legs: Specific Problem(s), Super Power, Sampling, and Stock Your Pond.
aliases: prospect to lead to coffee date pipeline
build a garden that attracts butterflies

concepts.name: Neediness Elimination
concepts.description: The principle that neediness is the biggest deal blocker. Having a steady stream of people waiting to do business with you eliminates neediness and dramatically improves closing ability. 'Needy people have skinny kids.'
aliases: neediness is the biggest deal blocker

concepts.name: Hero Auction
concepts.description: A strategy where businesses create irresistible 'fantasy packages' starting at $1 bids, using psychological triggers like curiosity, FOMO, social proof, and competitive bidding to emotionally invest potential customers. The real profit comes not from the auction winner but from following up with losing bidders who demonstrated buying intent.

concepts.name: Losing Bidders Goldmine
concepts.description: The insight that the auction is not about the winner -- it's a tool to identify every person who raised their hand and said 'I want that.' The losing bidders are pre-sold, emotionally invested leads who close at ~50% on backend offers, generating the bulk of revenue (e.g., $170K from one auction).
aliases: auction is not about the winner

concepts.name: CD3 Formula (Clearly Desirable x Clearly Doable x Clearly Different)
concepts.description: A framework for evaluating and sharpening an offer by scoring three dimensions: how strongly customers want it (Desirability), how achievable it feels (Doability), and how distinct it is from alternatives (Differentiation). Each scored 1-10. 'CLEARLY' means no thinking required.
aliases: clearly desirable clearly doable clearly different

concepts.name: The 5 Ps of Pre-Selling
concepts.description: A pre-selling framework built around five sequential steps (the 5 Ps) that guide prospects through a process before making an offer, so they're primed and ready to buy when you present it. Achieves 50-90% sales conversion rates.

concepts.name: Bottom Dollar Truth Serum
concepts.description: A framework for uncovering real motivations and objections that drive buying decisions through structured questioning. Uses carefully chosen language to create safety so people share honest information instead of defensive or false answers, enabling sharper messaging and higher-converting offers.

concepts.name: Buying Frenzy Offer Tool (BFOT)
concepts.description: A framework for designing compelling, easy-to-say-yes-to informational offers by framing them as a clear 'investment -> reward' exchange grounded in tangible outcomes. Lists concrete tasks/worries the audience would pay to eliminate, then ranks by urgency, emotional intensity, frequency of thought, and solution scarcity to find the strongest offer angle. 'Lane bumpers for bowling, but for offers.'
aliases: gimme blank and I'll give you blank
offer design tool for non-creative people

concepts.name: 2-Page Google Doc Cash Machine
concepts.description: A minimalist sales approach: create a micro-offer in a short Google Doc with a PayPal payment link, promote it with a simple social post, and drive quick sales. Designed for fast, low-effort testing of offers -- from blank doc to sales in 30 minutes.
aliases: blank doc to sales poppin in PayPal

concepts.name: Escape from Hell / Entrance into Heaven
concepts.description: A messaging and offer design principle that the most compelling copy and offers combine an escape from a painful current state (Hell) with arrival at a desirable future state (Heaven). Used to frame transformations in emails and offers.
aliases: escape from hell entrance into heaven
escape the 9 to 5 join the new rich

concepts.name: Coffee Date Model
concepts.description: A relationship-based sales approach where first meetings are structured as low-pressure 'coffee dates' (typically on Zoom) rather than hard pitches. Used for Beamer deals, auction deals, referral mojo, buying newsletters, communities, and off-market opportunities.

concepts.name: Newsletter Acquisition Strategy
concepts.description: A strategy for purchasing email newsletters as undervalued assets. Includes due diligence, dating (advertising in the newsletter first), understanding seller motivation, negotiating for transfer help and endorsement to existing subscribers. The acquisition is a 'no brainer' when combined with rainmaking skills.

concepts.name: Partner Promotion Interview
concepts.description: A strategy where Travis interviews and promotes a partner to his audiences for one year, pre-selling all 5 Ps, vouching for them, placing the interview in flow within Royalty Rockstars, and building a waiting list of pre-sold partners. Sold via auction format.

concepts.name: Old Playbook vs. New Playbook
concepts.description: A contrasting mental model: the old playbook of complicated funnels, frantic social media posting, and ad spend that leads to burnout with low-quality leads, versus the new approach of simple, psychology-driven strategies (like auctions) that generate pre-sold, high-intent buyers.

skills.name: Building Targeted Prospect Lists
skills.description: Rapidly building targeted prospect lists of people with a clear, timely problem you solve, using the Giving FUNnel assembly line process to generate prospects, convert them to leads, and fill your pipeline.

skills.name: Creating Irresistible Fantasy Auctions
skills.description: Designing an auction offer that combines curiosity, FOMO, and social proof into an irresistible package starting at $1, packaged in a way that emotionally invests potential customers before bidding begins.

skills.name: Running a Facebook Group Auction
skills.description: Executing a hero auction inside a Facebook group or similar community platform, managing bids, creating social proof through public bidding, and leveraging the competitive dynamics to identify hot leads.
aliases: run an auction in a Facebook group

skills.name: Following Up with Losing Bidders
skills.description: Making backend offers to auction participants who didn't win, leveraging their demonstrated buying intent and emotional investment to achieve ~50% close rates on follow-up offers.

skills.name: Scoring an Offer with CD3
skills.description: Rating your offer on Desirability, Doability, and Differentiation (each 1-10), identifying the weakest dimension, and adjusting positioning or promise to raise it so buying feels obvious and immediate.

skills.name: Writing a T1 Handraiser Email
skills.description: Crafting a simple email using the T1 template: state what's happening, highlight the clear benefit (escape from hell / entrance into heaven), and end with a direct invitation ('Would you like to join us?'). Used on your own list or others' lists.

skills.name: Creating a Microwave Offer in a Google Doc
skills.description: Whipping up a bite-sized micro-offer in a 2-page Google Doc with a PayPal link, posting a carefully chosen image and 2 sentences to a group, and generating sales within 30 minutes.

skills.name: Using the BFOT Worksheet to Design Offers
skills.description: Listing concrete tasks or worries your audience would pay to eliminate, then ranking them by urgency, emotional intensity, frequency of thought/talk, and solution scarcity to choose the strongest 'Gimme ___ and I'll give you ___' offer angle.

skills.name: Applying Truth Serum Questioning
skills.description: Using structured, safety-creating language to extract blunt, practical insights about real motivations and objections from prospects, turning those into sharper messaging and higher-converting offers. Avoiding 'wallet wrecker' phrasing that triggers defensiveness.

skills.name: Pre-Selling with the 5 Ps
skills.description: Intentionally guiding prospects through the five P steps before making an offer so they are primed, pre-sold, and ready to buy when you present the pitch, achieving 50-90% conversion rates.

skills.name: Purchasing an Email Newsletter
skills.description: Executing a newsletter acquisition: do research and due diligence, date the newsletter by advertising first, understand seller motivation, negotiate for transfer help and endorsement to subscribers, and close the deal quickly.

skills.name: Setting Up a Partner Waiting List
skills.description: Creating a waiting list of pre-sold partners so that whenever you have calendar space, you can press send on an email and fill it with piping hot coffee dates.
aliases: fill calendar with coffee dates on demand

skills.name: Delivering Value Before Selling (Sampling)
skills.description: Consistently delivering useful value (sampling your superpower) to targeted prospects until they convert into conversations and deals, rather than leading with a pitch.

contexts.name: Coffee Dates on Demand Course (CDODC)
contexts.description: A course teaching the Giving FUNnel framework for turning prospects into pre-sold leads and generating a steady stream of coffee date meetings.

contexts.name: Coffee Dates on Demand University (CDODU)
contexts.description: A university-level training program covering hero auctions, fantasy packages, bidding psychology, and advanced coffee date strategies.

contexts.name: Bottom Dollar Truth Serum (BDTS)
contexts.description: A training program on Travis Sago's truth serum framework for uncovering real buying motivations and crafting messaging that converts.

contexts.name: 3 Ways to Make $5K in 5 Days (3W5K)
contexts.description: A program teaching quick monetization strategies including the CD3 formula for sharpening offers to sell fast.

contexts.name: 2-Page Google Doc Cash Machine (2PG)
contexts.description: A training program on using the Buying Frenzy Offer Tool and 2-page Google Docs to create fast, simple offers that generate sales.

contexts.name: Hopper Millions (HOPM)
contexts.description: A beta program teaching the 5 Ps pre-selling framework and strategies for achieving high sales conversion rates.

contexts.name: Phoneless Sales Machine (PSM)
contexts.description: A program teaching email-based selling without phone calls, webinars, or videos -- including the Million Dollar Template Library with T1 handraiser emails and other scripts.

contexts.name: Bugatti 2-Page Cash Machine (B2P)
contexts.description: A program teaching the 2-page Google Doc cash machine approach for creating fast micro-offers with PayPal links.

contexts.name: Email Newsletter Purchase (ENP)
contexts.description: A training module demonstrating how to acquire email newsletters as assets, including due diligence, dating the newsletter, and negotiating the purchase.

contexts.name: 24-Hour Flash Auction (24HFA)
contexts.description: A community post series and strategy for running auction-style partner promotions, including partner interview endorsements sold via $1 minimum bid auctions.

contexts.name: Royalty Rockstars
contexts.description: Travis Sago's community of ~800+ members where partner interviews are placed 'in flow' and members connect for partnership opportunities.
